data_sources: OpenRouter public router leaderboard (proxied API call volumes)
_meta.methodology: Share figures are directional estimates blended from three publicly available developer-usage signals that measure different things: OpenRouter's public router leaderboard tracks API call volumes, Hugging Face counts downloads of open-weight checkpoints, and the Stack Overflow 2025 Developer Survey records self-reported usage. Because these axes are incommensurate (an API call is not a download is not a survey response), the resulting figure is best read as a normalized index of developer-facing usage rather than a precise market-share percentage. Coverage is restricted to the 10 tracked models in this snapshot; long-tail and niche models, private self-hosted enterprise deployments, and consumer-app usage without an API equivalent are not included. Numbers are rounded to whole percentages; the visible sum approximates 100% of tracked usage rather than 100% of the broader AI model market.

models.note: Declining as GPT-5 gains share; still largest single model by volume
High-volume low-cost workloads; slight pressure from Gemini Flash
Fastest growing in developer tooling and agentic coding pipelines
Growing share in Google Cloud and consumer app integrations
Open-weight deployments; dominant in self-hosted and on-premise use
Premium segment; strong in agentic and long-context tasks
Growing enterprise adoption; strong multimodal capabilities
Stable share; popular for cost-effective coding tasks
